channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Deadly alcohol poisoning casts shadow over the Laotian backpacker town
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A little town known as a backpacker paradise in northern Laos has come under spotlight for a mass poisoning case that has killed at least six tourists and potentially injured scores of others.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alabama carries out third nitrogen hypoxia execution in the country
item.description: Carey Dale Grayson, 50, fought his restraints and raised both middle fingers just before gas was administered, a witness said.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WGN 9

item.title: Human smugglers convicted after Indian family freezes to death trying to illegally cross northern border
item.description: Two men have been convicted of charges related to human smuggling in connection with a family of Indian migrants who froze to death in 2022 trying to cross into the U.S. from Canada.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jelly Roll pays tribute to Oklahoma teen who died from fentanyl
item.description: The 19-year-old died from a fentanyl overdose in May 2023, while attending Cameron University. Rain, who thought she was taking a Xanax, turned out the pill was laced with fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Mario Gonzalez/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: HEALDSBURG, Calif. (AP) — Heavy downpours fell over much of Northern California on Friday, causing small landslides, overflowing a river and flooding some streets, including in parts of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: NOAH BERGER and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump taps NFL player Scott Turner as head of HUD
item.description: Turner serves as the chair of the Center for Education Opportunity at America First Policy Institute, a Trump-aligned think tank.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: John Hopkins surgeon and COVID contrarian Marty Makary selected to lead Trump’s FDA
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Marty Makary, a Johns Hopkins's surgeon who has espoused contrarian views about the COVID-19 pandemic, to lead the Federal Drug Administration. Makary is a surgical oncologist at Johns Hopkins University and an author.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' third bid to be released on bail won't be decided until next week
item.description: A judge says he’ll rule next week on Sean “Diddy” Combs’ third request to be released from jail on bail while he awaits his sex trafficking trial.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Carolina GOP governor nominee vows to keep running after report on racial and sexual comments
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina Republican gubernatorial nominee Mark Robinson vowed on Thursday to remain in the race despite a CNN report that he posted strongly worded racial and sexual comments on an online message board, saying he won’t be forced out by “salacious tabloid lies.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and left his wife and three children has been located in Eastern Europe and is communicating with law enforcement, but he has not committed to returning home, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California officer shot, killed knife-wielding man after attack outside police headquarters: bodycam video
item.description: The Santa Monica Police Department released bodycam footage of a knife attack on an officer outside police headquarters that ended with the suspect being shot dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: Venezuelan migrant arrested in Colorado for allegedly sexually assaulting boss' 14-year-old daughter: report
item.description: A Venezuelan migrant is accused of sexually assaulting the 14-year-old daughter of his employer while he lived in the individual's house in Colorado, police and reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown: Denaturalization just a drop in the bucket
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming administration is expected to take aim at legal immigration, in addition to cracking down on the illegal variety, slowing the pace of application approvals and redirecting resources to look for fraud in old applications, including naturalizations.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KRON4

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church
item.description: It was suspected that the father had "orchestrated the disappearance and subsequent hiding" of the children.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, NewsNation

item.title: State of the Rio Grande: Officials discuss new sources of water from Mexico
item.description: Texas and Mexican officials held a seminar Thursday to hash out the current and future State of the Rio Grande as waters in the international river continue to dwindle, threatening border communities.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Senate reaches deal on stalled Biden judicial nominees
item.description: The Senate announced a deal Thursday to complete work on seven of President Biden’s nine stalled judicial nominees, who sparked an intense fight on the Senate floor this week.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American Airlines expands new software to improve boarding process
item.description: American Airlines is expanding new software to more than 100 airports to improve the boarding process.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elderly man accused of roommate and dog's 'brutal' murder had extensive criminal record
item.description: Austin police said an elderly man has been arrested for the "very brutal" deadly attack on his roommate and her dog, then using her credit cards and planning to flee the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of woman who went missing on cross-country vacation says cryptic texts sent from phone unlike her
item.description: Hannah Kobayashi never got on her flight from LA to NYC on Nov. 8 and disappeared in Los Angeles; new surveillance footage shows her with a mysterious man, her family says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your paycheck may be larger in 2025, even if you don't get a raise
item.description: While there's plenty of time left in November, you may already have something exciting to look forward to in 2025: a slightly larger paycheck, even without a raise.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon 'fundamentally rejects' ICC decision to issue arrest warrant for Netanyahu
item.description: The Pentagon rejected the International Criminal Court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Students can now apply for federal college aid. Officials hope for a smoother rollout this year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal FAFSA form used to apply for college financial aid opened to high school seniors nationwide on Thursday, a year after a disastrous rollout by the Biden administration delayed the application for months.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father of teen accused in Georgia school shooting pleads not guilty to charges against him
item.description: WINDER, Ga. (AP) — The father of a 14-year-old boy accused of carrying out a deadly mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia pleaded not guilty on Thursday to the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts are expecting the busiest Thanksgiving travel season ever
item.description: Nearly 80 million Americans will travel more than 50 miles for the upcoming Thanksgiving holiday, according to AAA, surpassing even pre-pandemic levels. That estimate would be an increase of 1.7 million people compared to last year and 2 million more than in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No charges filed against man arrested after teens set up sting
item.description: A 46-year-old California man ambushed by vigilante teens after he allegedly provided a girl with nude photos of himself and tried to arrange a sexual encounter with her is, so far, not facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4
